sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Sint-Truidense VV are in strong form with 6 consecutive wins and an unbeaten streak of 8 matches. KAA Gent, on the other hand, have struggled to secure wins recently, with 2 draws and 1 loss in their last 5 matches. Sint-Truidense's attacking prowess, led by Adriano Bertaccini (5 goals in last 5 matches), gives them an edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 2 head-to-head matches, KAA Gent have been dominant with 1 win and 1 draw, including a 2-0 victory in their most recent encounter. Gent have also scored first in all 6 of their last head-to-heads, showing early control in these matchups.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics & Trends
sections.text: Sint-Truidense VV have a strong home record with high-scoring games (over 2.5 goals in 8 of their last 10 matches). Gent’s away games often see both teams scoring (5 of 6 matches), but they’ve struggled to keep clean sheets. Card and corner statistics suggest low counts, aligning with trends of under 4.5 cards and 10.5 corners in most matches for both teams.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Sint-Truidense’s current winning streak and home advantage contrast with Gent’s inconsistent form. However, Gent’s historical edge in head-to-heads and ability to score first could play a role. The match is likely to be competitive with goals on both sides given recent trends.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the scoreline between Sint-Truidense VV and KAA Gent remains goalless at 0-0. Sint-Truidense VV has dominated possession and shown more intent in the attacking third, while KAA Gent has struggled to create meaningful chances. The home side appears to have the upper hand based on their statistical superiority in key areas, but the lack of clinical finishing keeps the game level.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team dominance
halftimeSections.text: Sint-Truidense VV has controlled much of the game so far, with significantly higher ball possession and a greater number of entries into the final third and penalty area. They've also been dominant in duels, winning a high percentage of both aerial and ground challenges. Despite only managing a single shot on target, their expected goals figure suggests they've created better opportunities compared to their opponents. Players like Andrés Ferrari and Ryotaro Ito have been active in creating chances, which could be key in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team struggles
halftimeSections.text: KAA Gent has had a challenging first half, with less possession and fewer entries into dangerous areas. They've failed to register a shot on target, and their expected goals figure is notably lower than Sint-Truidense VV's. Their duel-winning statistics are concerning, as they've been outmuscled in both aerial and ground battles. A yellow card to one of their players also hints at frustration or defensive pressure. However, players like Dante Vanzeir could pose a threat if they find space in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Sint-Truidense VV dominate the attacking momentum for large spells, particularly in the early and middle stages of the half, where they consistently pushed forward. KAA Gent had their moments, with a few attacking surges around the midpoint of the half, but their momentum often fizzled out. Towards the end of the half, Sint-Truidense VV regained control with sustained pressure, suggesting they might carry that intent into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Sint-Truidense VV, converting their dominance into goals will be crucial. Their ability to win duels and maintain possession in the final third gives them a solid platform to build on. KAA Gent, on the other hand, needs to improve their physicality in duels and find ways to create openings in Sint-Truidense VV's defense. Discipline will also be key for Gent, as avoiding further bookings could help them stay competitive.
